name: cf-commit

criteria.name: format_compliance
criteria.description: Commit message follows conventional commit format

scoring.0: No conventional commit format at all (missing type prefix)
scoring.1: Partial format (has type but missing scope or malformed description)
scoring.2: Correct format but scope could be more specific or accurate
scoring.3: Perfect conventional commit format with accurate type, scope, and description

criteria.name: scope_accuracy
criteria.description: Scope correctly identifies the affected package or area

scoring.0: No scope provided when one is clearly needed
scoring.1: Scope is too broad or generic (e.g., 'app' when 'cli' is more accurate)
scoring.2: Scope is reasonable but a more precise one exists
scoring.3: Scope precisely identifies the affected package/module/area

criteria.name: message_clarity
criteria.description: Subject line clearly communicates the why/what of the change

scoring.0: Subject is vague or misleading (e.g., 'update stuff', 'fix things')
scoring.1: Subject describes what changed but not why it matters
scoring.2: Subject is clear but could be more concise or specific
scoring.3: Subject is concise, specific, and immediately communicates the purpose

criteria.name: body_quality
criteria.description: Commit body provides useful context when the change warrants it

scoring.0: Complex change with no body explaining rationale
scoring.1: Body exists but is redundant with subject or lacks useful detail
scoring.2: Body provides context but misses key motivation or impact details
scoring.3: Body clearly explains why the change was made and any notable implications

notes: Simple changes (typos, formatting) may not need a body — score body_quality as 3 if omission is appropriate. Breaking changes must use ! suffix or BREAKING CHANGE footer.
